questions.question: Can I buy a set of clubs now and get them fitted later?
questions.answerSummary: Fitting is how you choose clubs, not a later add-on. Shaft (flex/material/weight) and head model are locked at purchase; lie angle, grip size, adjustable loft, and length can be adjusted after. Fit first at Clubworks, then buy.
questions.suggestedBitTopic: what a club fitting actually measures (flex, lie, length, grip) and which specs are locked at purchase vs adjustable later

questions.question: Full set under 90k, where to fit near Clover Greens, and what soft gear is still pending?
questions.answerSummary: Sub-88k pre-owned GI set (irons, used driver, one hybrid, 56 wedge, mallet putter). Fit at Clubworks (launch monitor), buy used at Golf Garage, new irons via GolfEdge. Pending soft gear: bag+headcovers, glove, balls, tools, collared polos.
questions.suggestedBitTopic: pre-owned vs new golf clubs — what's safe to buy used (driver, putter, irons) vs buy new (grips, wedges)

questions.question: Tailor an all-Callaway purchase list — does everything one brand look good or bad?
questions.answerSummary: One-brand reads as composed/intentional; Odyssey putter is Callaway-owned so a full Callaway/Odyssey bag has zero downside. All-Callaway sub-88k set given. Caveat: fit beats brand on the monitor.
questions.suggestedBitTopic: how club families/sets work — why matching irons+hybrid+wedge in one model line improves consistency

questions.question: Explain the fitting terms: launch monitor, gapping check PW to 56, head.
questions.answerSummary: Launch monitor = radar/camera that measures ball+club data. Gapping check = confirming even distance gaps between clubs, e.g. PW to 56 wedge. Head = the clubhead, chosen for forgiveness (game-improvement vs blade).
questions.suggestedBitTopic: fitting-shop vocabulary decoded: launch monitor, gapping, lie angle, and clubhead types

questions.question: I mishit more than I strike clean at the range — what should I do?
questions.answerSummary: Mishitting more than striking pure is normal at 110; game-improvement clubs exist for exactly this. Get a coach (Rs 1500-2500/session at Clover Greens/KGA) before fitting so launch-monitor data is meaningful. Range: one club (7-iron), half-swings first, slow tempo, aim at a target, quality over quantity. Most beginner mishits trace to grip, setup/posture, or swinging too hard.
questions.suggestedBitTopic: the three most common causes of mishits for beginners (grip, setup/posture, tempo) and a simple self-check for each

questions.question: I feel embarrassed taking lessons with my basic Decathlon set — won't the trainer judge me?
questions.answerSummary: Coaches judge coachability, not clubs; beginners are their core business. Decathlon Inesis is legit game-improvement gear, fine for lessons, and the package-set stigma is a peers-on-course thing, not a lesson-bay thing. Best sequence: learn on the Decathlon set (or range rentals) first, get fitted once the strike settles, then buy the Callaway set so you never tee up competitively with the starter set.
questions.suggestedBitTopic: why beginner gear is fine for lessons — coaches judge coachability, not clubs; when to learn first vs buy first
